definition: Appropriating views as something to hold, defend, or identify with; the view-member of the fourfold upādāna framework.
notes: The project now keeps taking views personally as the default because it aligns the compound more tightly with the upādāna headword while preserving the practical point: not merely having views, but appropriating them into identity and insistence. Clinging to views remains the familiar comparative alternate and is still acceptable in source-facing or continuity-sensitive prose. View appropriation and view-clinging are controlled explanatory alternates. The term should not be reduced to merely having opinions. It belongs to the four clinging-types framework and should be heard as a stronger, more appropriative movement than plain diṭṭhi. It also needs to stay distinct from attavādupādāna: the latter narrows the content to self-doctrine, while diṭṭhupādāna can range more broadly across held views. The OSF glossary supports the appropriative reading of this compound, and MN 11 supports its place in the fourfold upādāna structure.

context_rules.context: In source-facing or continuity-sensitive doctrinal prose using more familiar Buddhist-English terminology
context_rules.notes: Allowed alternate when continuity matters more than maximizing explicit alignment with the headword.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose emphasizing the appropriating function of upādāna
context_rules.notes: Use when the repository's broader upādāna logic should remain explicit.

context_rules.context: In compressed doctrinal prose or list form
context_rules.notes: Allowed compact alternate, not the default.

example_phrases.notes: One member of the fourfold set.
Shows the term as something relinquished rather than merely possessed.

version_notes: Retuned to align the compound more tightly with the upādāna family while preserving the familiar clinging alternate.

authority_basis.scope: Supports a strongly appropriative reading of the compound rather than reducing it to opinion-holding.
Supports the term's role as one member of the fourfold upādāna framework.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'taking views personally' as the default rendering in the fourfold upādāna framework unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Use 'clinging to views' only when continuity-sensitive prose or source-facing expectations make it the clearer choice. Do not reduce the term to mere opinions or beliefs.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift between plain view-language, upādāna-language, and the narrower self-doctrine compound.
